name: Alexander, Appenson
clubs: Member American Society of Mechanical Engineers (1916-17)
description: We are now in the heart of the "Flats," the abode of the doctor jockers, where silence and order reign supreme; but "Alex" has not arrived, for to those who know the presence of "Alex" there is a suspension of all work, unless the King of the Flats has some extraordinary work of importance to be done. Although "Alex" claims Marion as his home, we fear that his real address is a little more specific, and that he forgot to include "In case of the Asylum," but as superintendent. I am just nominally you puny P. M. L. T. R. Y. DUTY

name: Maxwell, Alexander Appel
description: "May" came to us from Albemarle, with much love for a caring life. His dreams are always of the fair sex, and when we find him hammering, "Carry Me Back to Pennsylvania." From his specialization in Chemistry, we once had a vision of his future in a Professor's chair, but, instead of this, "May" has chosen the fighting life for the present, and we are sure that he will rise among his army comrades. LOVE THE LADIES
text:
"May"
"May" came to us from Albemarle, with much love for a caring life. His dreams are always of the fair sex, and when we find him hammering, "Carry Me Back to Pennsylvania." From his specialization in Chemistry, we once had a vision of his future in a Professor's chair, but, instead of this, "May" has chosen the fighting life for the present, and we are sure that he will rise among his army comrades. LOVE THE LADIES

name: Banks, James Hall
clubs: Member American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: After exhausting the educational facilities of Norfolk, went out to Blackburg in order to study at the College of Knowledge. Upon his return he will be the first of the graduates who has not followed victory to his charms. While a student at the College of Knowledge, was interested in the study of finance and economics. He is a man who plays really for work and not for play. He is always ready to lend a hand to his friends and to help them in any way he can. He is a man who is always willing to help others and to give them the benefit of his experience and knowledge.

He is a man who is always willing to help others and to give them the benefit of his experience and knowledge.

name: Bell, James Alexander
description: Above shadow represents "Alex," a man small in stature, but in ability and in "all round" manliness, anything but small. Alex drifted across the hills from Staunton to Blacksburg in the fall of 1914. Since his earliest childhood days, his one ambition has been to locate himself in Birmingham immediately upon graduating. We leave it to you to decide from his commencement whether it is a case of chemical affinity or otherwise.
text:
"Poison"
Above shadow represents "Alex," a man small in stature, but in ability and in "all round" manliness, anything but small. Alex drifted across the hills from Staunton to Blacksburg in the fall of 1914. Since his earliest childhood days, his one ambition has been to locate himself in Birmingham immediately upon graduating. We leave it to you to decide from his commencement whether it is a case of chemical affinity or otherwise.

name: Beville, Meade
clubs: Member American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "Meade" lives up to the name of his home town in a fine way. He is an enterprising Crew (by himself) in class work. If an agreement arises, "Meade" will form his opinion, and that must be correct as if something like the "L.R.s" or "L.D. R.s" can prove the contrary. The "shirtwaists" have much attraction for "Meade", since he would never admit it. Just at present it is almost impossible to find him in his room, as there seems to be a great attraction down town. Perseverance will win, in any case, and "Meade" has that fine quality in abundance.

name: Gawry, Russell
description: They are wild, simply wild over me. So think it "Gawky." Anyway, that is the time he comes all the way from home to his country home every Sunday afternoon. He this gentleman seems to make good use of Price's Forks (as well as their special). Nevertheless, we can imagine nothing but success for "Gawky" in his chosen profession, although sometimes we stop to wonder how it is that he looks out for it: high to keep the eye of a train.

name: Branch, Lansley O'Conner
clubs: Member American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: The mathematics genius of the Junior Class; the distinctive feature of the intelligent "logarithm" is his; the greatest "three letter" in the universe; the swarthy "three letter" is the "three letter" in the universe; the swarthy "three letter" is the "three letter" in the universe.

name: Albemarle, Va.
motto: "Of the world Probably that is the land of 'Pop', but certainly by the record for dropping the first reveller. While not aspiring to military distinction, 'Pop' was the holder of an office until he decided that it did not suit his taste. To answer Uncle Sam's call, 'Pop' has rendered his service to the aerial branch of the service. This being the case, we should report to see him rise higher and higher."
description: Resigned in January to enter Aviation Corps.
text:
"Of the world Probably that is the land of 'Pop', but certainly by the record for dropping the first reveller. While not aspiring to military distinction, 'Pop' was the holder of an office until he decided that it did not suit his taste. To answer Uncle Sam's call, 'Pop' has rendered his service to the aerial branch of the service. This being the case, we should report to see him rise higher and higher."
Resigned in January to enter Aviation Corps.

name: Carrer, George Edward Jr.
description: Ladies, protect your hearts, for the twinkling of George's eyes has played havoc with the affections of many a fair young damsel. Prominent in all branches of student activities, George's influence has always made itself felt by the best in every field of school sports, whether athletic, literary, musical, or dramatic. Versatile, energetic, and the possessor of a pleasing personality, George will be an invaluable asset to the Army, which will claim his services, and his friends wish him many chances for further battle.
text: Ladies, protect your hearts, for the twinkling of George's eyes has played havoc with the affections of many a fair young damsel. Prominent in all branches of student activities, George's influence has always made itself felt by the best in every field of school sports, whether athletic, literary, musical, or dramatic. Versatile, energetic, and the possessor of a pleasing personality, George will be an invaluable asset to the Army, which will claim his services, and his friends wish him many chances for further battle.

name: Benjamins, Thomas Cocks
motto: "SO LONG AS I CAN HIDE BEHIND THE BUGLE I AM FREE TO PLAY ALL THE ANGELS I DESIRE"
description: "Ben," one of the brightest basketball stars of Tech's noted constellation, drilled in from Bristol presumably to elude the wishes of the delectable ladies of his native city. Chaste, loyal, and able, he soon won the respect of his fellow students, and during his absence in our midst, has been the inspiration of many brilliant victories. Until recently, his military accomplishments have been thoroughly negative, but he has shown great interest in human things. Our best wishes go with him.
text:

name: Davis, Guy
description: Martinsville is well known for its various and other things. Guy is one of the other things. He shared with us in the many talks and revelations of "Katland," and by his funny disposition and optimistic make-up, was the same as "Tulip." His military ambitions were realized in senior year, when he became a member of the gold team and "Tulip" is a patriotic, hard-working student and will always be held in high esteem by his classmates.

name: Dussell, Clarence Henry
description: We have the honor of introducing the man who is to be presented to Buckeye's Mechanics, that any other member of the Class, "Dixie," is the sort of fellow who always gets his work done well, but still spends plenty of time to do as he wishes. Among his other friends, we must mention the staging of a comedy revival, performing the Dixie Department, which has had a three years' standing and successful run here, with "Dixie" playing the role of the Buffalo. We wish you continued good luck, "Dixie." SAY, FELLERS, EVER HEAR THE ONE ABOUT THE BIG HEALTHY STIFF WHO WAS TOO PUNY FOR MILITARY? WELL, I "Dat"

name: Duncan, William Eustace
clubs: Member American Society of Mechanical Engineers (1916-17)
Member American Society of Mechanical Engineers (1917-18)
description: "Diggen," a British jolly in whom there is no guile! The first thing you notice about him is his ever-beaming smile. This smile is one of the kind that don't rub off, not even when he pulls a "T.V." under "Roscoe." Duncan is a "happy-go-lucky" fellow, who always looks on the bright side of things. His frank, open face and smiling countenance are always welcome from among the fair sex, and we predict that these same attributes will go a long way toward paving the way to success in future life. THE COLLEGE "PRETTY BOY" TO BE HEARD PLAYING THE ORGAN DURING CHURCH HOURS, IN POT YEA Y.M.C.A.

name: Gorman, June Wiley Jr.
description: It is by superstition that "Johnny" acquires the virtues and manners necessary to overcome the obstacles of life. In the past, "Johnny" has always had the best wishes, both as a bookworm and as a student; and now we wish him even better success at a future association for Uncle Sam.
text:
"Book-ages, newsboys, and dogs keep out."
It is by superstition that "Johnny" acquires the virtues and manners necessary to overcome the obstacles of life. In the past, "Johnny" has always had the best wishes, both as a bookworm and as a student; and now we wish him even better success at a future association for Uncle Sam.

name: Harrison, Archibald Chapman
description: He is a happy-go-lucky youngster, with a cheerful countenance and a smile which covers most of his face. His picture shows him to be a "Ladykiller." His cheery countenance indicates that he is a success in military life; his grades are proof conclusive that he is doubly a success in his class work. He has one fault: the complete inability to put any time on his books.

name: Ewris, Henry
motto: "You're wild, but he may be a little tough. 'Beef' is one of the keys from Norfolk, and Norfolk should be proud of him, for in his four years with the team he has done well in both military and classes. He is the team found in the room, and has never been caught studying. But he is one of the few that has a P. G. ticket. That's why we argue that there is no need of studying. His exam habit is going to ruin him on 'Bugle'. 'Bugle' is a very good excuse, but he is the Business Manager."
clubs: Executive Committee American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: YOU ARE MY MEAL TICKET—GO OUT AND GET ME THE BACON. THE BUGLE "Bugle" THE COLLEGE THE 1918 BUGLE

name: Hickman, Nathan Adam
description: He hails from the wilds of Rockbridge County, and tells a very interesting tale about the camp morning that he was going after the cows and heard the bugle blow. That is why he is here now. He is an intended farmer, for his legs were cut out for a horse's tank. His heart is bigger than himself, and he is always willing to lay aside his studies to correspond with the farmer.

He is an intended farmer, for his legs were cut out for a horse's tank. His heart is bigger than himself, and he is always willing to lay aside his studies to correspond with the farmer.

name: Jones, Alonzo Lewis
description: Yes, Sir; this is Alonzo. Yes, I have years and three summers in, has passed a B. S. Degree in these parts, and during the summer done a track course. Alonzo is the man who made a tentative Tech. give where an eight-page rule, and in this respect activities. Paraphrasing Randolph's definition of an Engineer. In every other respect, he is well. Alonzo is one of our best college spirit.
text:
"It's just for sure, Alonzo."
Yes, Sir; this is Alonzo. Yes, I have years and three summers in, has passed a B. S. Degree in these parts, and during the summer done a track course. Alonzo is the man who made a tentative Tech. give where an eight-page rule, and in this respect activities. Paraphrasing Randolph's definition of an Engineer. In every other respect, he is well. Alonzo is one of our best college spirit.

name: Krone, Faye Herman
motto: "When we entered the Freshman Class at Virginia Polytechnic Institute, we never thought that we had a classmate in our midst who would be Chief Garden Inspector and Adviser for the City of Roanoke. But when Roanoke asked for such a man, Floyd answered, as did he answer the many questions concerning the growing of apples which were asked him. We are proud to own him as a classmate, and expect great things of him."

We are proud to own him as a classmate, and expect great things of him."

name: Lancaster, George Graham
description: Graham joined as a Septimus, and has stuck around. He is specially to shake something, and sure to put something he is down to it. He has shown by his perseverance to work up to for example for he has the determination to see what in any taking. Of late, Graham has always his preference running an airplane rather than a train, for he has enlisted in the Aviation Corps. Our best wishes to with him.

name: Lark, James Willard
motto: "I'll open it"—you know what he meant. Not books, for despite his many stars "Birdie" had none. Nor his mouth, for that is always closed—that is why he knows more secrets than any other man in all the dry States. His heart? Well, in his heart he knew doubtless said "I'll open it," when Uncle Sam remarked the road to Berlin was closed, and "Birdie's" bar room tune can still be heard above many others "Over There," singing "I'll open it."
description: 1915-16—Out of Military; Sophomore Rat; Student Assistant in Office. 1916-17—Out of Military; Member German Club. 1917-18—Out of Military; Resigned in February to join Signal Corps, "Somewhere in France." COLLEGE OFFICE I HAVE YOUR PIC OF MY HOCKEY PIC AND I BEG TO PAY PIC "None"

name: Egin, Armenia
motto: "This little fellow, who talks from the wilds of Armenia, is small in stature, but great in intellect, he is one of the brilliant men of the Class. Joe is a Russian, and is one of the mainstays of the Band. He is also a hard worker, and spreads his spirit of time keeping. The Library is in order. We propose that Joe will turn out for the best, and with him the best of luck."
text: "This little fellow, who talks from the wilds of Armenia, is small in stature, but great in intellect, he is one of the brilliant men of the Class. Joe is a Russian, and is one of the mainstays of the Band. He is also a hard worker, and spreads his spirit of time keeping. The Library is in order. We propose that Joe will turn out for the best, and with him the best of luck."

name: Lynook, William Murray, Jr.
description: "Bill" hails from Blacksburg, and that is enough to discourage any man. "Bill," however, does not care a particle for environment, and in spite of his early handicap is making a place for himself everywhere he goes. He is one of the athletes of the Class. Being a star quickly, he is back on the Varsity. "Bill" also seems to be a star with the ladies, judging from the number of trips he has made to Farmville this year. One of "Bill's" ambitions is to join the Aviation Corps, and he predicts for himself a great future as one of Uncle Sam's fliers. We wish him the best of luck in his flight to Berlin.
text:
"Dern th' drill! That's me... ball!"
"Bill" hails from Blacksburg, and that is enough to discourage any man. "Bill," however, does not care a particle for environment, and in spite of his early handicap is making a place for himself everywhere he goes. He is one of the athletes of the Class. Being a star quickly, he is back on the Varsity. "Bill" also seems to be a star with the ladies, judging from the number of trips he has made to Farmville this year. One of "Bill's" ambitions is to join the Aviation Corps, and he predicts for himself a great future as one of Uncle Sam's fliers. We wish him the best of luck in his flight to Berlin.

name: Marshall, Harry Polkins Jones
description: He is, indeed, a true Virginia gentleman.
text: He is, indeed, a true Virginia gentleman.

name: Baxter, McEwen
clubs: Chairman American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: He is from Columbia, S. C., and truly lives up to his home address of Lady Street. He is always ready to do his duty. His willingness to help others has often caused him to be "turned" by his less industrious friends. He will always be remembered for his kindness and solemnness. His pet expression, when issuing orders, is, "You're right; I stand for order." He has always been a "warrior" in Military service, and if he continues to fight, he will undoubtedly succeed.
text: He is from Columbia, S. C., and truly lives up to his home address of Lady Street. He is always ready to do his duty. name: Morris, Charles Warr
description: Four years ago, Charles arrived from the "Huckleberry", and success has followed him ever since. The motto, "Actions speak louder than words," is certainly well exercised in the following: "Rally, dear Charles, crack a book, get at examination time the answers showered around him." This edition of "The Bugle" is a tribute to Charles' executive ability. We are sorry to see you leave us, Charles; but we feel sure you will continue to keep the interest of your alma mater at heart. Great things are expected of you; and we know we shall not be disappointed.
text:
"Actions speak louder than words,"
Four years ago, Charles arrived from the "Huckleberry", and success has followed him ever since. The motto, "Actions speak louder than words," is certainly well exercised in the following: "Rally, dear Charles, crack a book, get at examination time the answers showered around him." This edition of "The Bugle" is a tribute to Charles' executive ability. We are sorry to see you leave us, Charles; but we feel sure you will continue to keep the interest of your alma mater at heart. Great things are expected of you; and we know we shall not be disappointed.

name: Harnsberger, William
description: He is one who brings to mind the story of the "Hare and the Tortoise." He is the "butterfly" who always takes his time in everything, but always gets there. In the pitcher's box, this characteristic of him is particularly noticeable. On the other hand, when he is on the ballroom floor, among the ladies, there is nothing the heart desires but to be with him, as he is a delight to all.

name: Jons, Edward Orsosky
clubs: Member Student Branch American Society of Mechanical Engineers
Member American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: CLASS OF 1918 THE COLLEGE THE 1916 BUGLE
text: CLASS OF 1918 THE COLLEGE THE 1916 BUGLE

name: Oakes, Jinx Hillary
description: Jack entered our Class as a Sophomore Rat. He had just spent a year at Rensselaer College, etc. The change from civilian college life to a military career seemed to go hand in hand with him. He did not like the idea of getting up so early, and of answering to many long calls; but nevertheless he made good in military, and in his Junior year he was a sergeant. This session he thought that he would be a Senior Officer, but when he boldly told the military authorities that he had no military aspirations, he automatically became a "Fallen Angel."
text: Jack entered our Class as a Sophomore Rat. He had just spent a year at Rensselaer College, etc. The change from civilian college life to a military career seemed to go hand in hand with him. He did not like the idea of getting up so early, and of answering to many long calls; but nevertheless he made good in military, and in his Junior year he was a sergeant. This session he thought that he would be a Senior Officer, but when he boldly told the military authorities that he had no military aspirations, he automatically became a "Fallen Angel."

name: Pierce, Gustave Augustus, Jr.
description: "Ours" joined us as a Sophomore and has won more stars in classes and military during three years than the average man gets in four. He expects to enter the Aviation Corps in June, and we feel sure that his perseverance and level head will make him a leader in this work as in everything else he has attempted. THIS BUSINESS OF BEING AN OFFICER IS A SENIOR'S DUTY. WE OFFER THIS POST TENANT OF ATTITUDE TO THE COLLEGE AND THE 1918 BUGLE.

name: Pritchard, Claudius Harvey
motto: "I Just HUNG UP MY REPORT TO SERVE AS MY SERVICE FLAG"
text: "I Just HUNG UP MY REPORT TO SERVE AS MY SERVICE FLAG"

name: Percy
motto: "NEVER PHOTOGRAPH THE SHUTTER—LOOK AT THE MACHINE."
description: His military appearance easily distinguishes him from any of our noted local military heroes. One would never be sure that he has been exposed to the hardships of military training, for his every move and speech is non-military. This, however, should not lead one to believe that he is untrained. Had he only taken a little more interest in military as he does in photography, then he would have been our ranking captain, without a doubt.

name: Roubach, William Hoss
clubs: Treasurer of American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: He is a sober, good fellow, who hails from the Tidewater Section, and who is a happy combination of a musician, dancer, and scholar. A big heart and a business-like head make him one of our best. Four years have shown us that we have in this fellow a treasure of sound judgment, and a friend upon whom we can depend.

name: Smith, A. G.
motto: "Observing the Golden Rule; the true Virginia gentleman—such is my beloved 'A. G.'"
clubs: Member, Debating Team which defeated Virginia Military Institute
Secretary, Lee Literary Society, first term
President, Lee Literary Society, third term
President, Lee Literary Society, second term
description: Smith is a son of the "garden" country; a lover of nature; an expert gardener; his knowledge of Horticulture gained him the position of Assistant in that Department. In his long promotion in the military department, Smith certainly holds the record; he has risen to the rank of Lieutenant after a stay of three months in the ranks. He has also won many prizes as a student gardener.
text:
"Observing the Golden Rule; the true Virginia gentleman—such is my beloved 'A. G.'"
Smith is a son of the "garden" country; a lover of nature; an expert gardener; his knowledge of Horticulture gained him the position of Assistant in that Department. In his long promotion in the military department, Smith certainly holds the record; he has risen to the rank of Lieutenant after a stay of three months in the ranks. He has also won many prizes as a student gardener.

name: Smithson, Ernest Sheldrake
clubs: Executive Committee American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: Behold him who wears the crown in our military ranks. And a man could not be found who more perfectly embodies those qualities of leadership so necessary to the office which he holds. Besides being ranking Captain, "Prairie" is an excellent dancer, and a capable student. Judging from the place he holds in our hearts, we predict for him great future success.
text: Behold him who wears the crown in our military ranks. And a man could not be found who more perfectly embodies those qualities of leadership so necessary to the office which he holds. Besides being ranking Captain, "Prairie" is an excellent dancer, and a capable student. Judging from the place he holds in our hearts, we predict for him great future success.

name: Smith, Thomas Annews
description: "Quail" is so named because of his striking facial expression. Blazer to "Quail" Watson: It is said that this is the only similarity between these two noted V.P.I. characters. "Quail," however, is an expert rifleman, having scored a tally of twenty-five (denominative) shooting blanks in the dark. What would this boy wonder at watching him do in the trenches, with daylight and sure-enough bullets to help him? God help Kaiser Willie!

name: Southall, Copeland Savage
description: "Maiden" was "Kit's Mountain's" idea of a military garrison. This does not apply to "Maiden" with "Kit" Carver and Colonel Stone.
text: "Maiden" was "Kit's Mountain's" idea of a military garrison. This does not apply to "Maiden" with "Kit" Carver and Colonel Stone.

name: William, Brown St. Johns
motto: "I can't / Not well— / One leg's / Not strong / Just like / HELL. / 'St. Peter'"
text: "I can't / Not well— / One leg's / Not strong / Just like / HELL. / 'St. Peter'"

name: Taylor, Thomas Jolly
description: Jolly, by name and nature, entered the militia from the skirts of Richmond. The good in his academic work, military claimed a large share of his attention. His military career was auspiciously begun by the capture of a corporal in his September year. At the beginning of his Third Year, this budding Napoleon attained the rank of sergeant. He left us at the beginning of nineteen eighteen to enter the Training Camp, and we feel sure that he will be only too happy to return.

name: Hervy, Alvin
description: If the characteristics of this man could be converted into "dots" like eagles, he would lead John D. in his bank account by at least a billion. "Alvin" always has a smile and a handshake for everybody. He guards his future plans, but the daily walk indicates that Virginia Tech offers too many ways to a chemical of his characteristics.
text:
"Alvin"
If the characteristics of this man could be converted into "dots" like eagles, he would lead John D. in his bank account by at least a billion. "Alvin" always has a smile and a handshake for everybody. He guards his future plans, but the daily walk indicates that Virginia Tech offers too many ways to a chemical of his characteristics.

name: West, Jons Walter Jr.
clubs: Executive Committee American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: To be merely associated with "Johnny," you only know half of him. When you know him, he is as complicated as the World War. Sometimes he is as impulsive as a greengrocer's gun; at other times, a woman would delight in possessing him. Stubbornness, John loves music, and his ability to teach young ladies the song, "La La," is amazing. Nevertheless, he is a true lover. God bless him. You must meet John. SOME GIRL PLEASE WRITE THIS LONESOME BOY

name: Williams, William Reid Jr.
description: "Reid," who is one of Richmond's contributors, arrived here years ago, and has proved an asset to his Class in every way. He always has a good word for everybody, and as manager of this year's football team he has demonstrated his executive ability in more ways than one. We think he should be pursuing a course in Astronomy, for he seems very fond of certain varieties of "Keys," but the Agricultural work was more fortunate. Knowing his success in the past, we can only wish Reid continued good fortune in the future.
text:
"THE ATTENTION"
"Reid," who is one of Richmond's contributors, arrived here years ago, and has proved an asset to his Class in every way. He always has a good word for everybody, and as manager of this year's football team he has demonstrated his executive ability in more ways than one. We think he should be pursuing a course in Astronomy, for he seems very fond of certain varieties of "Keys," but the Agricultural work was more fortunate. Knowing his success in the past, we can only wish Reid continued good fortune in the future.
